title: Syracuse’s largest apartment complex has spiraled downward. Tenants have had enough
summary: Nob Hill Apartments, Syracuse's largest residential complex with 760 units, faces severe deterioration including non-functional elevators, inadequate heating, water damage, and broken locks. The city filed a lawsuit in April seeking $340,000 in fines for code violations; a new management company (Destra Multifamily) was hired in March to remediate, while tenants have organized demanding repairs by October 1 and rent freeze through 2026.

political_signal.description: Organized tenant action with 200+ petition signatures and scheduled news conference; concurrent city litigation creates multi-stakeholder pressure on property management to remediate housing quality violations.

processing_notes: Article truncated in source but contains sufficient detail for analysis
Lawsuit is pending as of article date (2025-08-27); city initiated enforcement action April 2025
Comparison cases mentioned: Skyline Apartments (Tim/Troy Green litigation 2023) and Lifestyle Apartments (2025 litigation, pending)
Dollar amount $58.5M represents 2018 purchase price by Windsor Capital Group; $340K represents cumulative fines as of April 2025
Tenant association formed January 2025; news conference scheduled for day after article date
New management (Labita/Destra) hired March 2025, suggesting remediation window is early-stage
